ansi(3) Library Functions Manual ansi(3)

NAME

ansi - ANSI Utilities

SYNOPSIS

Data Structures


struct ansi_rle_context_t
struct ansi_timing_t
struct rgb_error_t

Enumerations


enum ansi_color_mode_t { ANSI_MODE_FOREGROUND = 0, ANSI_MODE_BACKGROUND, ANSI_MODE_FOREGROUND_BACKGROUND }

Functions


char * ansi_strip_escapes (const char *input, size_t input_len)
Strip all ANSI escape sequences from a string. const char * ansi_skip_escape (const char *ptr, const char *end)
Find the end of an ANSI CSI escape sequence. bool ansi_is_already_colorized (const char *message, size_t pos)
Check if a position in text is already colored. char * append_truecolor_fg (char *dst, uint8_t r, uint8_t g, uint8_t b)
char * append_truecolor_bg (char *dst, uint8_t r, uint8_t g, uint8_t b)
char * append_truecolor_fg_bg (char *dst, uint8_t fg_r, uint8_t fg_g, uint8_t fg_b, uint8_t bg_r, uint8_t bg_g, uint8_t bg_b)
void ansi_rle_init (ansi_rle_context_t *ctx, char *buffer, size_t capacity, ansi_color_mode_t mode)
void ansi_rle_add_pixel (ansi_rle_context_t *ctx, uint8_t r, uint8_t g, uint8_t b, char ascii_char)
void ansi_rle_finish (ansi_rle_context_t *ctx)
void ansi_fast_init (void)
void ansi_fast_init_256color (void)
void ansi_fast_init_16color (void)
char * append_256color_fg (char *dst, uint8_t color_index)
char * append_256color_bg (char *dst, uint8_t color_index)
uint8_t rgb_to_256color (uint8_t r, uint8_t g, uint8_t b)
char * append_16color_fg (char *dst, uint8_t color_index)
char * append_16color_bg (char *dst, uint8_t color_index)
uint8_t rgb_to_16color (uint8_t r, uint8_t g, uint8_t b)
void get_16color_rgb (uint8_t color_index, uint8_t *r, uint8_t *g, uint8_t *b)
uint8_t rgb_to_16color_dithered (int r, int g, int b, int x, int y, int width, int height, rgb_error_t *error_buffer)
char * append_color_fg_for_mode (char *dst, uint8_t r, uint8_t g, uint8_t b, terminal_color_mode_t mode)

Detailed Description

Enumeration Type Documentation

enum ansi_color_mode_t

#include <include/ascii-chat/video/terminal/ansi.h>

Enumerator

ANSI_MODE_FOREGROUND
ANSI_MODE_BACKGROUND
ANSI_MODE_FOREGROUND_BACKGROUND

38              {
39   ANSI_MODE_FOREGROUND = 0,
40   ANSI_MODE_BACKGROUND,
41   ANSI_MODE_FOREGROUND_BACKGROUND,
42 } ansi_color_mode_t;










Function
  Documentation




void
  ansi_fast_init (void )


#include
    <include/ascii-chat/video/terminal/ansi.h>Initialize fast
    ANSI generation (call once at startup)


Definition at line 319 of file ansi.c.


319                           {
320   // Initialize the dec3 cache used by truecolor functions
321   ascii_simd_init();
322 }

    mirror_init_with_args(), and session_display_create().






void
  ansi_fast_init_16color (void )


#include
    <include/ascii-chat/video/terminal/ansi.h>Initialize
    16-color mode (thread-safe, called on first use)


Definition at line 398 of file ansi.c.


398                                   {
399   if (!lifecycle_init(&g_color16_lc, "ansi_16color")) {
400     return; // Already initialized
401   }
402   do_init_16color();
403 }

    session_display_create().

bool ansi_is_already_colorized (const char * message,
  size_t pos)


#include
    <include/ascii-chat/video/terminal/ansi.h>


Check if a position in text is already colored. Scans from the
    start of the message to the given position, tracking ANSI escape codes.
    Returns true if there is an active color code (not in reset state).




Detects reset codes: \x1b[0m and \x1b[m




Parameters


message The full message


pos Position to check


Returns


true if already colored (not in reset state), false if in
  reset state


Definition at line 98 of file ansi.c.


98                                                                 {
99   if (!message) {
100     return false;
101   }
102 
103   bool in_reset = true; // Start in reset state
104 
105   // Scan from beginning to current position looking for ANSI codes
106   for (size_t i = 0; i < pos && message[i] != '\0'; i++) {
107     if (message[i] == '\x1b' && message[i + 1] == '[') {
108       // Found ANSI escape sequence start
109       // Look for the end marker 'm'
110       size_t j = i + 2;
111       while (message[j] != '\0' && message[j] != 'm') {
112         j++;
113       }
114 
115       if (message[j] == 'm') {
116         // Extract the color code part (between [ and m)
117         // Check if it's a reset code: \x1b[0m or \x1b[m
118         in_reset = ((j == i + 3 && message[i + 2] == '0') || (j == i + 2));
119       }
120     }
121   }
122 
123   // Return true if NOT in reset state (already colorized)
124   return !in_reset;
125 }






Referenced by colorize_log_message().






void
  ansi_rle_add_pixel (ansi_rle_context_t * ctx, uint8_t r,
  uint8_t g, uint8_t b, char ascii_char)


#include
    <include/ascii-chat/video/terminal/ansi.h>Add pixel with
    run-length encoding (only emits SGR when color changes)


Parameters


ctx RLE context


r Red component


g Green component


b Blue component


ascii_char ASCII character to append


Definition at line 261 of file ansi.c.


261                                                                                                    {
262   // Check if we need to emit a new SGR sequence
263   bool color_changed = ctx->first_pixel || (r != ctx->last_r) || (g != ctx->last_g) || (b != ctx->last_b);
264 
265   if (color_changed) {
266     if (ctx->length + 40 >= ctx->capacity) {
267       // Log when we can't add a color change due to buffer capacity
268       log_warn_every(US_PER_SEC_INT, "RLE_CAPACITY: Cannot add SGR at length=%zu, capacity=%zu (need 40 bytes)",
269                      ctx->length, ctx->capacity);
270     }
271   }
272 
273   if (color_changed && (ctx->length + 40 < ctx->capacity)) { // Reserve 40 bytes for SGR (FG+BG max is 38)
274     char *pos = ctx->buffer + ctx->length;
275 
276     switch (ctx->mode) {
277     case ANSI_MODE_FOREGROUND:
278       pos = append_truecolor_fg(pos, r, g, b);
279       break;
280     case ANSI_MODE_BACKGROUND:
281       pos = append_truecolor_bg(pos, r, g, b);
282       break;
283     case ANSI_MODE_FOREGROUND_BACKGROUND:
284       // For FG+BG mode, use a default background (black) or implement dual-color logic
285       pos = append_truecolor_fg_bg(pos, r, g, b, 0, 0, 0);
286       break;
287     }
288 
289     ctx->length = (size_t)(pos - ctx->buffer);
290     ctx->last_r = r;
291     ctx->last_g = g;
292     ctx->last_b = b;
293     ctx->first_pixel = false;
294   }
295 
296   // Add the ASCII character
297   if (ctx->length < ctx->capacity - 1) {
298     ctx->buffer[ctx->length++] = ascii_char;
299   }
300 }






References ANSI_MODE_BACKGROUND,
    ANSI_MODE_FOREGROUND, ANSI_MODE_FOREGROUND_BACKGROUND,
    append_truecolor_bg(), append_truecolor_fg(),
    append_truecolor_fg_bg(), ansi_rle_context_t::buffer,
    ansi_rle_context_t::capacity, ansi_rle_context_t::first_pixel,
    ansi_rle_context_t::last_b, ansi_rle_context_t::last_g,
    ansi_rle_context_t::last_r, ansi_rle_context_t::length,
    log_warn_every, ansi_rle_context_t::mode, and
    US_PER_SEC_INT.


Referenced by image_print_color().






void
  ansi_rle_finish (ansi_rle_context_t * ctx)


#include
    <include/ascii-chat/video/terminal/ansi.h>Finish RLE
    sequence with reset and null terminator


Parameters


ctx RLE context


Definition at line 303 of file ansi.c.


303                                               {
304   // Add reset sequence (requires 4 bytes + 1 for null terminator = 5 bytes total)
305   if (ctx->length + 5 <= ctx->capacity) {
306     SAFE_MEMCPY(ctx->buffer + ctx->length, 4, "\033[0m", 4);
307     ctx->length += 4;
308   }
309 
310   // Null terminate
311   if (ctx->length < ctx->capacity) {
312     ctx->buffer[ctx->length] = '\0';
313   }
314 }






References ansi_rle_context_t::buffer,
    ansi_rle_context_t::capacity, ansi_rle_context_t::length, and
    SAFE_MEMCPY.


Referenced by image_print_color().






void
  ansi_rle_init (ansi_rle_context_t * ctx, char * buffer, size_t
  capacity, ansi_color_mode_t mode)


#include
    <include/ascii-chat/video/terminal/ansi.h>Initialize RLE
    context for efficient ANSI generation


Parameters


ctx Context to initialize


buffer Output buffer


capacity Buffer capacity


mode Color mode (foreground/background/both)


Definition at line 248 of file ansi.c.


248                                                                                                    {
249   ctx->buffer = buffer;
250   ctx->capacity = capacity;
251   ctx->length = 0;
252   ctx->mode = mode;
253   ctx->first_pixel = true;
254   // Initialize with impossible color values to force first SGR
255   ctx->last_r = 0xFF;
256   ctx->last_g = 0xFF;
257   ctx->last_b = 0xFF;
258 }






References ansi_rle_context_t::buffer,
    ansi_rle_context_t::capacity, ansi_rle_context_t::first_pixel,
    ansi_rle_context_t::last_b, ansi_rle_context_t::last_g,
    ansi_rle_context_t::last_r, ansi_rle_context_t::length, and
    ansi_rle_context_t::mode.


Referenced by image_print_color().






const
  char * ansi_skip_escape (const char * ptr, const char * end)


#include
    <include/ascii-chat/video/terminal/ansi.h>


Find the end of an ANSI CSI escape sequence. Given a pointer to an
    ESC character (\x1b) that starts a CSI sequence, returns a pointer past the
    final byte of the sequence. If the pointer does not point to a valid CSI
    sequence start, returns ptr + 1.




Parameters


ptr Pointer to the ESC (\x1b) character


end Pointer past the end of the string


Returns


Pointer to the first byte after the escape sequence


Definition at line 74 of file ansi.c.


74                                                                {
75   if (!ptr || ptr >= end || *ptr != '\x1b') {
76     return ptr ? ptr + 1 : ptr;
77   }
78 
79   const char *p = ptr + 1;
80   if (p >= end || *p != '[') {
81     return p; // Not a CSI sequence, skip just the ESC byte
82   }
83   p++; // Skip '['
84 
85   // Skip parameter bytes (0x30-0x3F) and intermediate bytes (0x20-0x2F)
86   while (p < end && ((*p >= 0x30 && *p <= 0x3F) || (*p >= 0x20 && *p <= 0x2F))) {
87     p++;
88   }
89 
90   // Skip final byte (0x40-0x7E)
91   if (p < end && *p >= 0x40 && *p <= 0x7E) {
92     p++;
93   }
94 
95   return p;
96 }






Referenced by truncate_with_ellipsis().






char
  * ansi_strip_escapes (const char * input, size_t input_len)


#include
    <include/ascii-chat/video/terminal/ansi.h>


Strip all ANSI escape sequences from a string. Removes all ANSI
    CSI sequences (ESC [ ... final_byte) from the input, leaving only printable
    text. Useful for creating plain text output from colorized ASCII art.




Parameters


input Input string containing ANSI escape
  sequences


input_len Length of input string


Returns


Newly allocated string with escapes removed (caller must
  free), or NULL on error


Definition at line 23 of file ansi.c.


23                                                               {
24   if (!input || input_len == 0) {
25     return NULL;
26   }
27 
28   // Allocate buffer directly for output (at most input_len bytes)
29   char *output = SAFE_MALLOC(input_len + 1, char *);
30   if (!output) {
31     return NULL;
32   }
33 
34   size_t out_pos = 0;
35   size_t i = 0;
36 
37   while (i < input_len) {
38     // Check for ESC character (start of ANSI sequence)
39     if (input[i] == '\x1b' && i + 1 < input_len && input[i + 1] == '[') {
40       // Skip ESC[
41       i += 2;
42 
43       // Skip parameter bytes (digits, semicolons, and intermediate bytes)
44       while (i < input_len) {
45         char c = input[i];
46         // Parameter bytes: 0x30-0x3F (digits, semicolon, etc.)
47         // Intermediate bytes: 0x20-0x2F (space, !, ", etc.)
48         if ((c >= 0x30 && c <= 0x3F) || (c >= 0x20 && c <= 0x2F)) {
49           i++;
50         } else {
51           break;
52         }
53       }
54 
55       // Skip final byte (0x40-0x7E: @, A-Z, [, \, ], ^, _, `, a-z, {, |, }, ~)
56       if (i < input_len) {
57         char final = input[i];
58         if (final >= 0x40 && final <= 0x7E) {
59           i++;
60         }
61       }
62     } else {
63       // Regular character - copy to output
64       output[out_pos++] = input[i];
65       i++;
66     }
67   }
68 
69   // Null-terminate the output
70   output[out_pos] = '\0';
71   return output;
72 }






References SAFE_MALLOC.


Referenced by display_height(), display_width(),
    layout_print_two_column_row(), and log_mmap_write().






char
  * append_16color_bg (char * dst, uint8_t color_index)


#include
    <include/ascii-chat/video/terminal/ansi.h>Append 16-color
    ANSI background SGR sequence


Parameters


dst Destination buffer


color_index 16-color index (0-15)


Returns


Pointer to end of written data


Definition at line 421 of file ansi.c.


421                                                         {
422   // Ensure initialization before use
423   ansi_fast_init_16color();
424 
425   if (color_index >= 16) {
426     color_index = 0; // Default to black background
427   }
428 
429   const char *color_str = color16_bg_strings[color_index];
430   while (*color_str) {
431     *dst++ = *color_str++;
432   }
433 
434   return dst;
435 }

    image_print_16color_dithered_with_background(), and
    rgb_to_16color_halfblocks_scalar().

408

411   }
412 
413   const char *color_str = color16_fg_strings[color_index];

416   }
417

char
  * append_color_fg_for_mode (char * dst, uint8_t r, uint8_t g,
  uint8_t b, terminal_color_mode_t mode)


#include
    <include/ascii-chat/video/terminal/ansi.h>Append color SGR
    sequence for the appropriate terminal color mode


Parameters


dst Destination buffer


r Red component


g Green component


b Blue component


mode Terminal color mode capability


Returns


Pointer to end of written data


Definition at line 588 of file ansi.c.


588                                                                                                        {
589   switch (mode) {
590   case COLOR_MODE_TRUECOLOR:
591     return append_truecolor_fg(dst, r, g, b);
592 
593   case COLOR_MODE_256_COLOR: {
594     uint8_t color_index = rgb_to_256color(r, g, b);
595     return append_256color_fg(dst, color_index);
596   }
597 
598   case COLOR_MODE_16_COLOR: {
599     uint8_t color_index = rgb_to_16color(r, g, b);
600     return append_16color_fg(dst, color_index);
601   }
602 
603   case COLOR_MODE_NONE:
604   case COLOR_MODE_AUTO:
605   default:
606     // No color output for monochrome mode or auto mode (fallback)
607     return dst;
608   }
609 }






References append_16color_fg(),
    append_256color_fg(), append_truecolor_fg(),
    COLOR_MODE_16_COLOR, COLOR_MODE_256_COLOR,
    COLOR_MODE_AUTO, COLOR_MODE_NONE, COLOR_MODE_TRUECOLOR,
    rgb_to_16color(), and rgb_to_256color().






char
  * append_truecolor_bg (char * dst, uint8_t r, uint8_t g,
  uint8_t b)


#include
    <include/ascii-chat/video/terminal/ansi.h>Append truecolor
    background SGR sequence Maximum output: 19 bytes
    (\033[48;2;255;255;255m)


Parameters


dst Destination buffer


r Red component


g Green component


b Blue component


Returns


Pointer to end of written data


Definition at line 173 of file ansi.c.


173                                                                       {
174   // Ensure dec3 cache is initialized
175   if (!g_dec3_cache.dec3_initialized) {
176     init_dec3();
177   }
178 
179   SAFE_MEMCPY(dst, 19, "\033[48;2;", 7);
180   dst += 7;
181 
182   SAFE_MEMCPY(dst, 12, g_dec3_cache.dec3_table[r].s, g_dec3_cache.dec3_table[r].len);
183   dst += g_dec3_cache.dec3_table[r].len;
184   *dst++ = ';';
185 
186   SAFE_MEMCPY(dst, 8, g_dec3_cache.dec3_table[g].s, g_dec3_cache.dec3_table[g].len);
187   dst += g_dec3_cache.dec3_table[g].len;
188   *dst++ = ';';
189 
190   SAFE_MEMCPY(dst, 4, g_dec3_cache.dec3_table[b].s, g_dec3_cache.dec3_table[b].len);
191   dst += g_dec3_cache.dec3_table[b].len;
192   *dst++ = 'm';
193 
194   return dst;
195 }

Parameters


dst Destination buffer


r Red component


g Green component


b Blue component


Returns


Pointer to end of written data


Definition at line 143 of file ansi.c.


143                                                                       {
144   // Ensure dec3 cache is initialized
145   if (!g_dec3_cache.dec3_initialized) {
146     init_dec3();
147   }
148 
149   // Static prefix - 7 bytes
150   SAFE_MEMCPY(dst, 19, "\033[38;2;", 7);
151   dst += 7;
152 
153   // Red component + semicolon
154   SAFE_MEMCPY(dst, 12, g_dec3_cache.dec3_table[r].s, g_dec3_cache.dec3_table[r].len);
155   dst += g_dec3_cache.dec3_table[r].len;
156   *dst++ = ';';
157 
158   // Green component + semicolon
159   SAFE_MEMCPY(dst, 8, g_dec3_cache.dec3_table[g].s, g_dec3_cache.dec3_table[g].len);
160   dst += g_dec3_cache.dec3_table[g].len;
161   *dst++ = ';';
162 
163   // Blue component + suffix
164   SAFE_MEMCPY(dst, 4, g_dec3_cache.dec3_table[b].s, g_dec3_cache.dec3_table[b].len);
165   dst += g_dec3_cache.dec3_table[b].len;
166   *dst++ = 'm';
167 
168   return dst;
169 }

char
  * append_truecolor_fg_bg (char * dst, uint8_t fg_r, uint8_t
  fg_g, uint8_t fg_b, uint8_t bg_r, uint8_t bg_g,
  uint8_t bg_b)


#include
    <include/ascii-chat/video/terminal/ansi.h>Append combined
    foreground + background truecolor SGR Maximum output: 38 bytes
    (\033[38;2;255;255;255;48;2;255;255;255m)


Parameters


dst Destination buffer


fg_r Foreground red component


fg_g Foreground green component


fg_b Foreground blue component


bg_r Background red component


bg_g Background green component


bg_b Background blue component


Returns


Pointer to end of written data


Definition at line 199 of file ansi.c.


200                                            {
201   // Ensure dec3 cache is initialized
202   if (!g_dec3_cache.dec3_initialized) {
203     init_dec3();
204   }
205 
206   SAFE_MEMCPY(dst, 38, "\033[38;2;", 7);
207   dst += 7;
208 
209   // Foreground RGB (remaining: 31 bytes max)
210   SAFE_MEMCPY(dst, 31, g_dec3_cache.dec3_table[fg_r].s, g_dec3_cache.dec3_table[fg_r].len);
211   dst += g_dec3_cache.dec3_table[fg_r].len;
212   *dst++ = ';';
213 
214   // Remaining: 27 bytes max
215   SAFE_MEMCPY(dst, 27, g_dec3_cache.dec3_table[fg_g].s, g_dec3_cache.dec3_table[fg_g].len);
216   dst += g_dec3_cache.dec3_table[fg_g].len;
217   *dst++ = ';';
218 
219   // Remaining: 23 bytes max
220   SAFE_MEMCPY(dst, 23, g_dec3_cache.dec3_table[fg_b].s, g_dec3_cache.dec3_table[fg_b].len);
221   dst += g_dec3_cache.dec3_table[fg_b].len;
222 
223   // Background RGB (remaining: 20 bytes max)
224   SAFE_MEMCPY(dst, 20, ";48;2;", 6);
225   dst += 6;
226 
227   // Remaining: 14 bytes max
228   SAFE_MEMCPY(dst, 14, g_dec3_cache.dec3_table[bg_r].s, g_dec3_cache.dec3_table[bg_r].len);
229   dst += g_dec3_cache.dec3_table[bg_r].len;
230   *dst++ = ';';
231 
232   // Remaining: 10 bytes max
233   SAFE_MEMCPY(dst, 10, g_dec3_cache.dec3_table[bg_g].s, g_dec3_cache.dec3_table[bg_g].len);
234   dst += g_dec3_cache.dec3_table[bg_g].len;
235   *dst++ = ';';
236 
237   // Remaining: 6 bytes max
238   SAFE_MEMCPY(dst, 6, g_dec3_cache.dec3_table[bg_b].s, g_dec3_cache.dec3_table[bg_b].len);
239   dst += g_dec3_cache.dec3_table[bg_b].len;
240   *dst++ = 'm';
241 
242   return dst;
243 }






References g_dec3_cache, init_dec3(), and
    SAFE_MEMCPY.


Referenced by ansi_rle_add_pixel().






void
  get_16color_rgb (uint8_t color_index, uint8_t * r,
  uint8_t * g, uint8_t * b)


#include
    <include/ascii-chat/video/terminal/ansi.h>Get RGB values for
    a 16-color ANSI index


Parameters


color_index 16-color index (0-15)


r Output red component


g Output green component


b Output blue component


Definition at line 480 of file ansi.c.


480                                                                               {
481   // Same color table as rgb_to_16color()
482   static const uint8_t ansi_colors[16][3] = {
483       {0, 0, 0},       // 0: Black
484       {128, 0, 0},     // 1: Dark Red
485       {0, 128, 0},     // 2: Dark Green
486       {128, 128, 0},   // 3: Dark Yellow (Brown)
487       {0, 0, 128},     // 4: Dark Blue
488       {128, 0, 128},   // 5: Dark Magenta
489       {0, 128, 128},   // 6: Dark Cyan
490       {192, 192, 192}, // 7: Light Gray
491       {128, 128, 128}, // 8: Dark Gray
492       {255, 0, 0},     // 9: Bright Red
493       {0, 255, 0},     // 10: Bright Green
494       {255, 255, 0},   // 11: Bright Yellow
495       {0, 0, 255},     // 12: Bright Blue
496       {255, 0, 255},   // 13: Bright Magenta
497       {0, 255, 255},   // 14: Bright Cyan
498       {255, 255, 255}  // 15: White
499   };
500 
501   if (color_index >= 16) {
502     color_index = 7; // Default to light gray
503   }
504 
505   *r = ansi_colors[color_index][0];
506   *g = ansi_colors[color_index][1];
507   *b = ansi_colors[color_index][2];
508 }






Referenced by
    image_print_16color_dithered_with_background(), and

uint8_t rgb_to_16color (uint8_t r, uint8_t
  g, uint8_t b)


#include
    <include/ascii-chat/video/terminal/ansi.h>Convert RGB to
    closest 16-color ANSI index Uses distance-based matching against standard
    ANSI colors


Parameters


r Red component


g Green component


b Blue component


Returns


16-color index (0-15)


Definition at line 437 of file ansi.c.


437                                                         {
438   // Convert RGB to the closest 16-color ANSI color
439   // This uses a simple distance-based approach
440 
441   // Define the 16 ANSI colors in RGB
442   static const uint8_t ansi_colors[16][3] = {
443       {0, 0, 0},       // 0: Black
444       {128, 0, 0},     // 1: Dark Red
445       {0, 128, 0},     // 2: Dark Green
446       {128, 128, 0},   // 3: Dark Yellow (Brown)
447       {0, 0, 128},     // 4: Dark Blue
448       {128, 0, 128},   // 5: Dark Magenta
449       {0, 128, 128},   // 6: Dark Cyan
450       {192, 192, 192}, // 7: Light Gray
451       {128, 128, 128}, // 8: Dark Gray
452       {255, 0, 0},     // 9: Bright Red
453       {0, 255, 0},     // 10: Bright Green
454       {255, 255, 0},   // 11: Bright Yellow
455       {0, 0, 255},     // 12: Bright Blue
456       {255, 0, 255},   // 13: Bright Magenta
457       {0, 255, 255},   // 14: Bright Cyan
458       {255, 255, 255}  // 15: White
459   };
460 
461   uint8_t best_match = 0;
462   int min_distance = INT_MAX;
463 
464   for (int i = 0; i < 16; i++) {
465     int dr = (int)r - (int)ansi_colors[i][0];
466     int dg = (int)g - (int)ansi_colors[i][1];
467     int db = (int)b - (int)ansi_colors[i][2];
468     int distance = dr * dr + dg * dg + db * db;
469 
470     if (distance < min_distance) {
471       min_distance = distance;
472       best_match = i;
473     }
474   }
475 
476   return best_match;
477 }






Referenced by append_color_fg_for_mode(),
    colorscheme_compile_scheme(), image_print_16color(),
    rgb_to_16color_dithered(), and
    rgb_to_16color_halfblocks_scalar().






uint8_t rgb_to_16color_dithered (int r, int g, int b, int
  x, int y, int width, int height, rgb_error_t * error_buffer)


#include
    <include/ascii-chat/video/terminal/ansi.h>Convert RGB to
    16-color with Floyd-Steinberg dithering


Parameters


r Red component


g Green component


b Blue component


x Current pixel X coordinate


y Current pixel Y coordinate


width Image width


height Image height


error_buffer Dithering error buffer (width*height elements)


Returns


16-color index with dithering applied


Definition at line 511 of file ansi.c.


511                                                                                                                      {
512   // Add accumulated error from previous pixels
513   if (error_buffer) {
514     // Use size_t for index calculation to prevent integer overflow on large images
515     size_t error_idx = (size_t)y * (size_t)width + (size_t)x;
516     r += error_buffer[error_idx].r;
517     g += error_buffer[error_idx].g;
518     b += error_buffer[error_idx].b;
519 
520     // Reset error for this pixel
521     error_buffer[error_idx].r = 0;
522     error_buffer[error_idx].g = 0;
523     error_buffer[error_idx].b = 0;
524   }
525 
526   // Clamp values to [0, 255]
527   uint8_t r_clamped = clamp_rgb((int)r);
528   uint8_t g_clamped = clamp_rgb((int)g);
529   uint8_t b_clamped = clamp_rgb((int)b);
530 
531   // Find the closest 16-color match
532   uint8_t closest_color = rgb_to_16color(r_clamped, g_clamped, b_clamped);
533 
534   // Calculate quantization error if dithering is enabled
535   if (error_buffer) {
536     uint8_t actual_r, actual_g, actual_b;
537     get_16color_rgb(closest_color, &actual_r, &actual_g, &actual_b);
538 
539     int error_r = r - (int)actual_r;
540     int error_g = g - (int)actual_g;
541     int error_b = b - (int)actual_b;
542 
543     // Distribute error using Floyd-Steinberg weights:
544     //     * 7/16
545     // 3/16 5/16 1/16
546 
547     // Error to right pixel (x+1, y)
548     // Use size_t for all index calculations to prevent integer overflow
549     if (x + 1 < width) {
550       size_t right_idx = (size_t)y * (size_t)width + (size_t)(x + 1);
551       error_buffer[right_idx].r += (error_r * 7) / 16;
552       error_buffer[right_idx].g += (error_g * 7) / 16;
553       error_buffer[right_idx].b += (error_b * 7) / 16;
554     }
555 
556     // Error to pixels on next row (y+1)
557     if (y + 1 < height) {
558       // Bottom-left pixel (x-1, y+1)
559       if (x - 1 >= 0) {
560         size_t bl_idx = (size_t)(y + 1) * (size_t)width + (size_t)(x - 1);
561         error_buffer[bl_idx].r += (error_r * 3) / 16;
562         error_buffer[bl_idx].g += (error_g * 3) / 16;
563         error_buffer[bl_idx].b += (error_b * 3) / 16;
564       }
565 
566       // Bottom pixel (x, y+1)
567       size_t bottom_idx = (size_t)(y + 1) * (size_t)width + (size_t)x;
568       error_buffer[bottom_idx].r += (error_r * 5) / 16;
569       error_buffer[bottom_idx].g += (error_g * 5) / 16;
570       error_buffer[bottom_idx].b += (error_b * 5) / 16;
571 
572       // Bottom-right pixel (x+1, y+1)
573       if (x + 1 < width) {
574         size_t br_idx = (size_t)(y + 1) * (size_t)width + (size_t)(x + 1);
575         error_buffer[br_idx].r += (error_r * 1) / 16;
576         error_buffer[br_idx].g += (error_g * 1) / 16;
577         error_buffer[br_idx].b += (error_b * 1) / 16;
578       }
579     }
580   }
581 
582   return closest_color;
583 }






References rgb_error_t::b, rgb_error_t::g,
    get_16color_rgb(), rgb_error_t::r, and
    rgb_to_16color().


Referenced by image_print_16color_dithered(), and
    image_print_16color_dithered_with_background().






uint8_t rgb_to_256color (uint8_t r, uint8_t
  g, uint8_t b)


#include
    <include/ascii-chat/video/terminal/ansi.h>Convert RGB to
    closest 256-color palette index Uses 6x6x6 color cube + grayscale
  ramp


Parameters


r Red component


g Green component


b Blue component


Returns


256-color palette index (0-255)


Definition at line 360 of file ansi.c.


360                                                          {
361   // Map to 6x6x6 color cube (216 colors) + grayscale ramp
362 
363   // Check if it's close to grayscale
364   int avg = (r + g + b) / 3;
365   int gray_diff = abs(r - avg) + abs(g - avg) + abs(b - avg);
366 
367   if (gray_diff < 30) {
368     // Use grayscale ramp (colors 232-255)
369     int gray_level = (avg * 23) / 255;
370     return (uint8_t)(232 + gray_level);
371   }
372 
373   // Use 6x6x6 color cube (colors 16-231)
374   int r6 = (r * 5) / 255;
375   int g6 = (g * 5) / 255;
376   int b6 = (b * 5) / 255;
377 
378   return (uint8_t)(16 + (r6 * 36) + (g6 * 6) + b6);
379 }






Referenced by append_color_fg_for_mode(),
    colorscheme_compile_scheme(), image_print_256color(), and
    rgb_to_256color_halfblocks_scalar().

Zachary Fogg <me@zfo.gg>

    ascii-chat
